question: Find all the content about Reflector — which protocols integrate it?

golden.answer:
Run a dated **exact-entity content lookup** for Reflector and return the sourced rows by content type; use semantic search only as adjacent discovery and label it accordingly. A live 2026-07-10 corpus read returned Reflector-related articles, audiovisual items, governance proposals, and SCF submissions, so a static project profile is not an 'all content' answer.

For integration context, Stellar's official oracle page listed **Blend, OrbitCDP, DeFindex, Laina, EquitX, Slender, and SorobanDomains**. Reflector's operator page additionally declared **Etherfuse, Normal, Stratum, and xBidAI**. Keep the official-seven and provider-eleven rosters separate, date them, and do not infer that every listing is a current dependency.

Include the February 22 YieldBlox incident only with the verified boundary: a manipulated thin USTRY/USDC SDEX market dominated Reflector Pulse's five-minute VWAP in fresh rounds, and the consuming YieldBlox pool accepted the values. No Reflector-contract compromise was established. Do not claim dominance without a reproducible metric.
keyFacts: Returns a dated, sourced exact-entity content set and distinguishes it from semantic adjacent discovery.
Separates the dated official-seven integration roster from the provider-declared eleven-name roster.
Describes the YieldBlox incident without claiming a Reflector contract compromise.
avoid: Do NOT substitute one project profile for the requested content set or present semantic neighbors as exact matches.
Do NOT fabricate or hard-gate integrations without a dated, source-attributed roster.
Do NOT claim Reflector itself was hacked or that a five-minute VWAP made thin-market pricing manipulation-proof.
Do NOT assert an unsupported dominance/de-facto ranking.
golden.notes:
All-content-for-one-project → lumenloop_find_content_about_project (this is the content/news lane; the reverse-dependency 'who depends on Reflector' query is q-defi-reflector-related-projects → lumenloop_get_related_projects, so get_related_projects removed from acceptable here to keep lanes distinct). Grounded on Scout: Reflector = decentralized price oracle / data-feed network for Stellar & Soroban via a DAO-run node quorum, powering DeFi protocols such as Blend, Laina, and Slender (dossier also lists Orbit CDP, EquitX, DeFindex). YieldBlox tie-in VERIFIED 2026-06-29 — the Feb 22 2026 (~00:25 UTC) YieldBlox/Blend V2 USTRY oracle-manipulation incident (~$10.2M, ~$7.2M/48M XLM frozen by Tier-1 validators) is multi-sourced (Halborn, QuillAudits, Olympix, Protos, Script3, Reflector). Reflector's thin-liquidity VWAP USTRY feed was the exploited price source; root cause was the consuming pool operator's feed config, NOT Reflector core (Reflector quoted correct prices for the data it observed).
Also good if the answer separates exact-entity content from semantic discovery and distinguishes official-listed, provider-declared, and code-evidenced relationships.
Golden cites: Each surfaced item carries its source.
GT-17 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: content and rosters are freshness-sensitive. Require sourced exact-entity rows, source-relative roster tiers, and the market/feed/consumer incident boundary; no Reflector-compromise or dominance claim.

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The answer used an unsupported de-facto superlative and treated one static profile as fulfillment of an all-content request. GT-17 independently confirmed the incident boundary but found that current exact-entity corpus results, semantic discovery, the official seven-name roster, and the provider-declared eleven-name roster must remain distinct.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-09 primary/blind incident matrices and GT-17 primary process 3247 / blind process 3248 content matrices.
rootCause: A static directory/profile record was treated as an all-content answer and a dated roster as timeless/exhaustive.
Causal-chain shorthand implied a Reflector compromise.
